Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Claude `remote-control` CLI feature:

## What is Remote Control?

Remote Control connects claude.ai/code or the Claude app for iOS and Android to a Claude Code session running on your machine. You can start a task at your desk, then pick it up from your phone on the couch or a browser on another computer.

## How It Works

Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only and never opens inbound ports on your machine. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the web or mobile client and your local session over a streaming connection. All traffic travels through the Anthropic API over TLS.

## Key Features

Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available. The terminal generates a unique session URL and a QR code for your mobile device. Once you open that link on your phone, tablet, or another browser, the two surfaces stay in perfect sync—allowing you to start a task at your desk and continue it from the couch while maintaining full access to your local filesystem and project configuration.

## Requirements

Remote Control requires a Pro or Max plan. API keys are not supported. You need to run claude and use /login to sign in through claude.ai if you haven't already.

## Getting Started

Run claude remote-control to start a new session.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code Remote Control:

## Overview

Anthropic has introduced a new Claude Code feature called Remote Control, allowing developers to continue a local coding session from a phone, tablet, or any web browser. On February 25, 2026, Anthropic shipped the feature every Claude Code user has been waiting for: Remote Control — the ability to kick off a coding task in your terminal, put your laptop in your bag, and keep full control from your phone.

## How It Works

Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available. Claude Code on the web executes in Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure. Your files and MCP servers never leave your machine; only the chat messages and tool results flow through the encrypted bridge.

## Getting Started

Simply navigate to your project directory and run claude remote-control to initialize the session. The terminal will then generate a unique session URL and a QR code (toggleable via the spacebar) for your mobile device.

## Availability and Limitations

Currently, Remote Control is available as a Research Preview for subscribers on the Claude Max tier. While access for Claude Pro ($20/month) users is expected shortly, the feature remains a high-end tool for power users and is notably absent from Team or Enterprise plans during this initial phase.

Users can only run one remote session at a time; terminal must stay open; and if a machine is unable to reach a network for more than "roughly 10 minutes," the session will time out.

> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Continue local sessions from any device with Remote Control

> Continue a local Claude Code session from your phone, tablet, or any browser using Remote Control. Works with claude.ai/code and the Claude mobile app.

<Note>
  Remote Control is available as a research preview on Pro and Max plans. It is not available on Team or Enterprise plans.
</Note>

Remote Control connects [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) or the Claude app for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) and [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude) to a Claude Code session running on your machine. Start a task at your desk, then pick it up from your phone on the couch or a browser on another computer.

When you start a Remote Control session on your machine, Claude keeps running locally the entire time, so nothing moves to the cloud. With Remote Control you can:

* **Use your full local environment remotely**: your filesystem, [MCP servers](/en/mcp), tools, and project configuration all stay available
* **Work from both surfaces at once**: the conversation stays in sync across all connected devices, so you can send messages from your terminal, browser, and phone interchangeably
* **Survive interruptions**: if your laptop sleeps or your network drops, the session reconnects automatically when your machine comes back online

Unlike [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web), which runs on cloud infrastructure, Remote Control sessions run directly on your machine and interact with your local filesystem. The web and mobile interfaces are just a window into that local session.

This page covers setup, how to start and connect to sessions, and how Remote Control compares to Claude Code on the web.

## Requirements

Before using Remote Control, confirm that your environment meets these conditions:

* **Subscription**: requires a Pro or Max plan. API keys are not supported.
* **Authentication**: run `claude` and use `/login` to sign in through claude.ai if you haven't already.
* **Workspace trust**: run `claude` in your project directory at least once to accept the workspace trust dialog.

## Start a Remote Control session

You can start a new session directly in Remote Control, or connect a session that's already running.

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="New session">
    Navigate to your project directory and run:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    claude remote-control
    ```

    The process stays running in your terminal, waiting for remote connections. It displays a session URL you can use to [connect from another device](#connect-from-another-device), and you can press spacebar to show a QR code for quick access from your phone. While a remote session is active, the terminal shows connection status and tool activity.

    This command supports the following flags:

    * **`--verbose`**: show detailed connection and session logs
    * **`--sandbox`** / **`--no-sandbox`**: enable or disable [sandboxing](/en/sandboxing) for filesystem and network isolation during the session. Sandboxing is off by default.
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="From an existing session">
    If you're already in a Claude Code session and want to continue it remotely, use the `/remote-control` (or `/rc`) command:

    ```
    /remote-control
    ```

    This starts a Remote Control session that carries over your current conversation history and displays a session URL and QR code you can use to [connect from another device](#connect-from-another-device). The `--verbose`, `--sandbox`, and `--no-sandbox` flags are not available with this command.

    <Tip>
      Use `/rename` before running `/remote-control` to give the session a descriptive name. This makes it easier to find in the session list across devices.
    </Tip>
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

### Connect from another device

Once a Remote Control session is active, you have a few ways to connect from another device:

* **Open the session URL** in any browser to go directly to the session on [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code). Both `claude remote-control` and `/remote-control` display this URL in the terminal.
* **Scan the QR code** shown alongside the session URL to open it directly in the Claude app. With `claude remote-control`, press spacebar to toggle the QR code display.
* **Open [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) or the Claude app** and find the session by name in the session list. Remote Control sessions show a computer icon with a green status dot when online.

The remote session takes its name from your last message, your `/rename` value, or "Remote Control session" if there's no conversation history. If the environment already has an active session, you'll be asked whether to continue it or start a new one.

If you don't have the Claude app yet, use the `/mobile` command inside Claude Code to display a download QR code for [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/claude-by-anthropic/id6473753684) or [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.anthropic.claude).

### Enable Remote Control for all sessions

By default, Remote Control only activates when you explicitly run `claude remote-control` or `/remote-control`. To enable it automatically for every session, run `/config` inside Claude Code and set **Enable Remote Control for all sessions** to `true`. Set it back to `false` to disable.

Each Claude Code instance supports one remote session at a time. If you run multiple instances, each one gets its own environment and session.

## Connection and security

Your local Claude Code session makes outbound HTTPS requests only and never opens inbound ports on your machine. When you start Remote Control, it registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work. When you connect from another device, the server routes messages between the web or mobile client and your local session over a streaming connection.

All traffic travels through the Anthropic API over TLS, the same transport security as any Claude Code session. The connection uses multiple short-lived credentials, each scoped to a single purpose and expiring independently.

## Remote Control vs Claude Code on the web

Remote Control and [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web) both use the claude.ai/code interface. The key difference is where the session runs: Remote Control executes on your machine, so your local MCP servers, tools, and project configuration stay available. Claude Code on the web executes in Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure.

Use Remote Control when you're in the middle of local work and want to keep going from another device. Use Claude Code on the web when you want to kick off a task without any local setup, work on a repo you don't have cloned, or run multiple tasks in parallel.

## Limitations

* **One remote session at a time**: each Claude Code session supports one remote connection.
* **Terminal must stay open**: Remote Control runs as a local process. If you close the terminal or stop the `claude` process, the session ends. Run `claude remote-control` again to start a new one.
* **Extended network outage**: if your machine is awake but unable to reach the network for more than roughly 10 minutes, the session times out and the process exits. Run `claude remote-control` again to start a new session.

## Related resources

* [Claude Code on the web](/en/claude-code-on-the-web): run sessions in Anthropic-managed cloud environments instead of on your machine
* [Authentication](/en/authentication): set up `/login` and manage credentials for claude.ai
* [CLI reference](/en/cli-reference): full list of flags and commands including `claude remote-control`
* [Security](/en/security): how Remote Control sessions fit into the Claude Code security model
* [Data usage](/en/data-usage): what data flows through the Anthropic API during local and remote sessions

## Claude Code Remote Control: Complete Guide

**Remote Control** is a feature that allows you to continue a local Claude Code session running on your machine from your phone, tablet, or any web browser. It's essentially the ability to "disconnect" from your terminal and control the same session remotely while keeping all local tools, MCP servers, and filesystem access available.

### What is Remote Control?

Remote Control connects claude.ai/code or the Claude mobile app (iOS/Android) to a Claude Code session running locally on your machine. Your code execution stays on your machine—nothing moves to the cloud. This is fundamentally different from Claude Code on the web, which runs in Anthropic's cloud infrastructure.

**Key distinction**: Remote Control = local execution + remote interface | Claude Code on web = cloud execution

### Requirements

Before using Remote Control, you need:

1. **Subscription**: Claude Pro ($20/month) or Max plan
   - Not available on Team or Enterprise plans
   - API keys are not supported
   - You must use `/login` to authenticate through claude.ai

2. **Authentication**: Run `claude` and sign in with `/login` if you haven't already

3. **Workspace trust**: Run `claude` in your project directory at least once to accept the workspace trust dialog

### How to Start Remote Control

#### Option 1: Start a New Session

Navigate to your project directory and run:

```bash
claude remote-control
```

**Flags supported:**
- `--verbose`: Show detailed connection and session logs
- `--sandbox` / `--no-sandbox`: Enable or disable filesystem/network isolation (off by default)

The terminal will display:
- A session URL (copy/paste into browser)
- A QR code (press spacebar to toggle visibility for mobile scanning)
- Real-time connection status and tool activity

The process stays running in your terminal waiting for remote connections.

#### Option 2: Convert Existing Session to Remote

If you're already in an active Claude Code session, use:

```
/remote-control
```

Or the shorthand:

```
/rc
```

This converts your current session (with all conversation history) to Remote Control mode and displays the session URL and QR code.

**Pro tip**: Use `/rename` before `/remote-control` to give the session a descriptive name for easier discovery across devices.

#### Option 3: Enable for All Sessions

To enable Remote Control automatically for every session:

```
/config
```

Then set **Enable Remote Control for all sessions** to `true`. Set to `false` to disable.

### Connecting from Another Device

Once Remote Control is active, connect from:

1. **Browser**: Open the session URL directly in any browser (takes you to claude.ai/code)
2. **Mobile**: Scan the QR code with the Claude app, or open claude.ai/code and find the session by name
3. **Session picker**: Open claude.ai/code or Claude app and locate the session in your session list (shows a computer icon with green status dot when online)

Remote Control sessions are named based on:
- Your last message in the session
- Your `/rename` value (if set)
- Default: "Remote Control session"

### How It Works: Connection & Security

**Network architecture:**
- Your local Claude Code session makes only outbound HTTPS requests
- No inbound ports are opened on your machine
- When you start Remote Control, the session registers with the Anthropic API and polls for work
- The Anthropic API server routes messages between your remote device and local session over an encrypted streaming connection
- All traffic travels through the Anthropic API over TLS (same as regular Claude Code sessions)

**Security details:**
- Multiple short-lived credentials, each scoped to a single purpose
- Each credential expires independently
- Your local filesystem and MCP servers never leave your machine—only chat messages and tool results flow through the encrypted bridge
- Session automatically reconnects if your network drops or machine sleeps

### Remote Control vs Claude Code on the Web

| Aspect | Remote Control | Claude Code on Web |
|--------|---|---|
| **Execution location** | Your local machine | Anthropic cloud infrastructure |
| **Access to local tools** | Yes (MCP servers, filesystem, config) | No |
| **Use case** | Continue existing work from another device | Start new work without local setup |
| **Performance** | Local execution speed | Cloud resources |
| **Setup required** | Must have Claude Code installed locally | None |

### Important Limitations

1. **One remote session per Claude Code instance**: Each local Claude Code session supports only one active Remote Control connection at a time

2. **Terminal must stay open**: Remote Control runs as a local process. If you close the terminal or stop the `claude` process, the session ends. You must run `claude remote-control` again to restart

3. **Network timeout**: If your machine is awake but unable to reach the network for more than ~10 minutes, the session times out and the process exits. Run `claude remote-control` again to reconnect

4. **Not available on Enterprise/Team plans**: Currently research preview for Pro/Max subscribers only

### Real-World Example Workflow

```bash
# At your desk: start a coding task
cd ~/my-project
claude remote-control

# Terminal output:
# Session URL: https://claude.ai/code/session/...
# QR Code displayed (press spacebar to show/hide)
# Status: Connected and waiting

# From your couch: scan QR code with Claude app
# Or open the session URL in a browser
# Continue the exact same session with full access to your project

# Your laptop can sleep safely—session reconnects automatically when it wakes
```

### Setting up for Remote Work

```bash
# Give your session a memorable name before going remote
/rename "Build Authentication System"

# Then enable remote control
/remote-control

# Optionally enable for all future sessions
/config
# Then toggle "Enable Remote Control for all sessions" to true
```

### Security Considerations

- Only make outbound connections (pull-based, not push)
- No inbound ports opened on your machine
- All credentials are temporary and purpose-scoped
- Your local environment (secrets, filesystem, MCP servers) stays on your machine
- The same TLS security as any Claude Code session
- Works with Claude Code's permission system and sandboxing options

---

content.text:
## Claude Code Remote Control

Voici un guide complet sur la fonctionnalité **Remote Control** de Claude Code :

### Qu'est-ce que c'est ?

Remote Control permet de **contrôler une session Claude Code locale** depuis votre téléphone, tablette ou navigateur web via claude.ai/code ou l'app mobile Claude. Votre code s'exécute toujours localement — seuls les messages transitent via le serveur Anthropic.

### Comment l'utiliser

**Démarrer une nouvelle session remote :**
```bash
claude remote-control
```

**Convertir la session en cours** (depuis une session Claude Code active) :
```
/remote-control
```
ou le raccourci :
```
/rc
```

> `/remote-control` est une **commande intégrée** de Claude Code CLI — je ne peux pas l'exécuter pour vous depuis cette session. Tapez-la directement dans votre terminal.

### Options disponibles

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--verbose` | Logs détaillés de connexion |
| `--sandbox` / `--no-sandbox` | Active/désactive l'isolation filesystem |

### Prérequis

- **Abonnement** Claude Pro ou Max (pas Team/Enterprise)
- **Authentification** via `/login` (pas de clé API)
- Avoir accepté le **workspace trust** au moins une fois dans le projet

### Comment ça marche

1. La session locale s'enregistre auprès de l'API Anthropic (connexion sortante HTTPS uniquement)
2. Un **URL de session** et un **QR code** s'affichent dans le terminal
3. Scannez le QR code ou ouvrez l'URL depuis un autre appareil
4. Vous contrôlez la même session avec accès complet à vos fichiers locaux, serveurs MCP, etc.

### Points importants

- Le **terminal doit rester ouvert** — si vous fermez le processus, la session s'arrête
- **Une seule connexion remote** par session à la fois
- Timeout réseau de ~10 min si la machine perd la connectivité
- La session se **reconnecte automatiquement** après un sleep/réveil

### Workflow typique

```bash
# Au bureau
cd ~/mon-projet
claude remote-control
# → URL + QR code affichés

# Depuis le canapé : scannez le QR code avec l'app Claude
# ou ouvrez l'URL dans un navigateur
# → Continuez exactement la même session
```

Pour activer Remote Control **par défaut** sur toutes vos sessions, utilisez `/config` et activez l'option correspondante.

```bash
claude --version
```

```bash
claude remote-control
```

C'est étrange. Votre config est correcte (Max + claude.ai), mais la fonctionnalité est bloquée. Quelques pistes :

### Causes possibles

1. **Déploiement progressif** : Remote Control est en "research preview" et n'a peut-être pas encore été activé pour tous les comptes Max. Le rollout est progressif.

2. **Région** : La fonctionnalité pourrait ne pas être disponible dans toutes les régions.

3. **Apple Private Relay** : Votre email utilise `privaterelay.appleid.com` — il est possible (peu probable) que cela interfère avec la vérification du plan.

### Actions recommandées

1. **Vérifiez sur claude.ai** : Allez sur [claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code) dans votre navigateur et voyez si l'option Remote Control apparaît dans l'interface web.

2. **Contactez le support Anthropic** : Si la fonctionnalité n'apparaît pas non plus sur le web, ouvrez un ticket à support@anthropic.com en mentionnant que vous avez un plan Max et que Remote Control n'est pas disponible.

3. **Vérifiez votre abonnement** : Allez sur [claude.ai/settings/billing](https://claude.ai/settings/billing) pour confirmer que le plan Max est bien actif.

La fonctionnalité étant très récente (lancée le 25 février 2026), il est probable qu'elle soit en cours de déploiement progressif.

content.text:
Voilà le problème potentiel : on est **à l'intérieur d'une session Claude Code**, ce qui empêche de lancer des commandes `claude` imbriquées.

### Ce que vous devez faire

**Depuis un terminal séparé** (pas depuis cette session Claude Code) :

1. **Rafraîchissez votre authentification** :
   ```bash
   claude logout
   claude login
   ```

2. **Puis lancez Remote Control** :
   ```bash
   claude remote-control
   ```

3. **Ou depuis une session Claude Code** (dans un autre terminal) :
   ```
   /remote-control
   ```
   ou
   ```
   /rc
   ```

Le message d'erreur "not yet available on your plan" pourrait être un bug lié à un token d'auth expiré ou à un cache local. La ré-authentification devrait résoudre le problème.

Si ça persiste après le re-login, essayez aussi de vider le cache :
```bash
rm -rf ~/.claude/cache
claude login
claude remote-control
```
